packets.body:
## Identity

## Edges


## Origins

When the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings) breaks out after the death of King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), his brother [Renly](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) marries [Margaery Tyrell](wiki:Margaery_Tyrell) at [Highgarden](wiki:Highgarden) and claims the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) with the support of [House Tyrell](wiki:House_Tyrell).(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) Margaery's father, Lord [Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell), is eager for her to be a queen,(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) and he also wants to be the grandfather of a king.(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Rasos6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) [Renly is assassinated](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(299)), however, leaving Margaery a widow.(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Racok33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)
Robert's brother [Stannis](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) is defeated in the [Battle of the Blackwater](wiki:Battle_of_the_Blackwater),(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Racok62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) and Margaery agrees to marry Robert's supposed son, King [Joffrey I Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon), as part of an alliance between Houses Tyrell and [Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister).(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Racok65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) After Joffrey chokes to death during [their wedding feast](wiki:Purple_Wedding) in the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep),(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Rasos60.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) the Tyrells insist that Margaery wed Joffrey's younger brother, Prince [Tommen Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon).(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Rasos67.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) At the [Fingers](wiki:Fingers), Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) tells [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) that he had conspired with Margaery's grandmother, Lady [Olenna Tyrell](wiki:Olenna_Tyrell), to assassinate the volatile Joffrey so that Margaery can marry Joffrey's younger brother, Prince [Tommen Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon).(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)
During [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister)'s trial for murder, Grand Maester [Pycelle](wiki:Pycelle) testifies that Joffrey was [poisoned](wiki:Strangler).(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) Tyrion, falsely convicted of killing Joffrey, murders [Shae](wiki:Shae) and his father, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), in the [Tower of the Hand](wiki:Tower_of_the_Hand) before disappearing from the city.(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Rasos77.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)
Although [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End) is held by Stannis's men, Mace refuses to depart [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) until Margaery weds King Tommen.(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) Tommen's mother, the increasingly-paranoid Queen Regent [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister), prefers a betrothal and thinks the marriage should be delayed for a year or two. She reluctantly agrees to a wedding to avoid offending [Highgarden](wiki:Highgarden), however.(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) The Tyrells insist against Cersei's wishes that Margaery and Tommen share a bed, with Cersei agreeing that they can do so for just the wedding night. Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister), [Lord Commander of the Kingsguard](wiki:Lord_Commander_of_the_Kingsguard) and the actual father of Joffrey and Tommen, explains to his sister Cersei that Margaery's cousins—[Megga](wiki:Megga_Tyrell), [Alla](wiki:Alla_Tyrell), and [Elinor Tyrell](wiki:Elinor_Tyrell)—will be with the married youths that night, but Cersei orders Jaime to be in the room as well.(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)
Cersei wants Tommen to wear the red silk cloak which Joffrey had worn at his wedding, it was the same cloak which Cersei's father, Lord Tywin, had used when he married [Joanna Lannister](wiki:Joanna_Lannister). Olenna objects to Tommen wearing the colors of House Lannister, however, and the boy king instead wears the heavy cloth-of-gold cloak worn by Robert [when he married Cersei](wiki:Wedding_of_Robert_I_Baratheon_and_Cersei_Lannister).(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

## Aftermath

Lord Tyrell departs King's Landing to [besiege Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(300)),(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-13)(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) his mother Olenna returns to [Highgarden](wiki:Highgarden),(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-14) and his son, Ser [Garlan Tyrell](wiki:Garlan_Tyrell), marches to claim [Brightwater Keep](wiki:Brightwater_Keep).(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-15) Ser [Kevan Lannister](wiki:Kevan_Lannister) travels to [Darry](wiki:Darry) to see his son Lancel wed to [Amerei Frey](wiki:Amerei_Frey).(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-16) Jaime leads a host to bring order to the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands), including [Riverrun](wiki:Siege_of_Riverrun).(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)
Lady Merryweather becomes Cersei's confidant.(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) The Queen Regent plots against Queen Margaery, eventually having her accused of adultery.(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Raffc43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)

## Quotes

Lady [Olenna](wiki:Olenna_Tyrell) was not about to let [Joff](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon) harm her precious darling granddaughter, but unlike [her son](wiki:Mace_Tyrell) she also realized that under all his flowers and finery, Ser [Loras](wiki:Loras_Tyrell) is as hot-tempered as [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister). Toss Joffrey, [Margaery](wiki:Margaery_Tyrell), and Loras in a pot, and you've got the makings for kingslayer stew. The old woman understood something else as well. Her son was determined to make Margaery a queen, and for that he needed a king ... but he did not need Joffrey. We shall have another wedding soon, wait and see. Margaery will marry [Tommen](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon). She'll keep her queenly crown and her [maidenhead](wiki:Maidenhead), neither of which she especially wants, but what does that matter? The great western alliance will be preserved ... for a time, at least.(wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)
Cersei: [Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(300))? Yes, but ... [Lord Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell) has made it tediously plain that he will not leave [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) till [Tommen](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) marries [Margaery](wiki:Margaery_Tyrell). Jaime: Then let them wed. It 

...[TRUNCATED — body exceeds 8000 char cap]...

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: War of the Five Kings (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: House Manderly captures Hornwood but later withdraws, resulting in a Bolton victory (track_b: Result)
- DEFEATS: Ramsay Snow believed dead after intervention of Winterfell (track_b: Result)

## Aftermath

Despite the supposed death of Ramsay, the northerners of Winterfell still fear his father, Roose Bolton, will press a claim on the Hornwood lands. Rodrik also states that fighting is ongoing between Bolton and Manderly men as he lacks a strong enough force of men to stop them.(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) Rodrik imprisons "Reek"—actually Ramsay—in Winterfell's dungeon.(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1) "Reek" is taken into the service of [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy) during the [capture of Winterfell](wiki:Capture_of_Winterfell).(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Theon eventually sends him to gather reinforcements, resulting in Ramsay and Bolton men slaying Rodrik, White Harbor knights and Hornwoods, along with other northern [Stark](wiki:House_Stark) loyalists, in a [battle at Winterfell](wiki:Battle_at_Winterfell). Theon allows Ramsay into Winterfell, resulting in the slaughtering of the ironborn garrison of Winterfell and [sacking](wiki:Sack_of_Winterfell) the [winter town](wiki:Winter_town).(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

Following the betrayal of House Stark by the Boltons and Freys at the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding), the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) legitimizes Ramsay and names his father, Lord Roose Bolton, [Warden of the North](wiki:Warden_of_the_North).(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Rasos72.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) Ramsay presents himself from this point onwards as [Lord of the Hornwood](wiki:Lord_of_the_Hornwood) and heir to the Dreadfort.(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)

Because Wyman Manderly's son and heir, Ser [Wylis](wiki:Wylis_Manderly), was captured at the [Trident](wiki:Fighting_at_the_fords_of_the_Trident), Wyman agrees to a deal with the [Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister). In exchange for Wyman executing the imprisoned Lord [Davos Seaworth](wiki:Davos_Seaworth), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King) and envoy of [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), as well as Wyman withdrawing from the Hornwood lands and castles and submitting to Roose, Wylis will be released from [Maidenpool](wiki:Maidenpool).(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Raffc17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Raffc24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd58.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0) Sometime after this agreement is made the Manderlys withdraw from Hornwood territory, allowing for the Boltons to take control of the region.

Whilst some Hornwoods submit to Ramsay and attend his wedding to "[Arya Stark](wiki:Jeyne_Poole)",(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-1) other Hornwood survivors of the Bolton betrayal at Winterfell join Stannis following his victorious [fight by Deepwood Motte](wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte).(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)

## Quotes

> [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton)'s [bastard](wiki:Ramsay_Snow) had started it by seizing [Lady Hornwood](wiki:Donella_Hornwood) as she returned from the [harvest feast](wiki:Harvest_feast), marrying her that very night even though he was young enough to be her son. Then [Lord Manderly](wiki:Wyman_Manderly) had taken [her castle](wiki:Hornwood). To protect the Hornwood holdings from the [Boltons](wiki:House_Bolton), he had written, but Ser [Rodrik](wiki:Rodrik_Cassel) had been almost as angry with him as with the bastard.(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)
>
> —thoughts of [Bran Stark](wiki:Bran_Stark)

> [He](wiki:Reek) is the only witness to the worst of [the Bastard's](wiki:Ramsay_Snow) crimes. Perhaps when Lord [Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton) hears his tale, he will abandon his claim, but meantime we have [Manderly](wiki:White_Harbor) knights and [Dreadfort](wiki:Dreadfort) men killing one another in [Hornwood forests](wiki:Hornwood_(forest)), and I lack the strength to stop them.(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2)
> —[Rodrik Cassel](wiki:Rodrik_Cassel) to [Luwin](wiki:Luwin)

> **Rhaegar:** Have it as you will. By any name, [he](wiki:Ramsay_Snow) shall soon be wed to [Arya Stark](wiki:Jeyne_Poole). If you would keep faith with your promise, give him your allegiance, for he shall be your [Lord of Winterfell](wiki:Lord_of_Winterfell). **Wylla:** He won't ever be my lord! He made [Lady Hornwood](wiki:Donella_Hornwood) marry him, then shut her in a dungeon and made her eat her fingers.(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-1)
> —[Rhaegar Frey](wiki:Rhaegar_Frey) and [Wylla Manderly](wiki:Wylla_Manderly) in the [Merman's Court](wiki:Merman's_Court)

## Aftermath

Despite the supposed death of Ramsay, the northerners of Winterfell still fear his father, Roose Bolton, will press a claim on the Hornwood lands. Rodrik also states that fighting is ongoing between Bolton and Manderly men as he lacks a strong enough force of men to stop them.(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) Rodrik imprisons "Reek"—actually Ramsay—in Winterfell's dungeon.(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1) "Reek" is taken into the service of [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy) during the [capture of Winterfell](wiki:Capture_of_Winterfell).(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok46.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Theon eventually sends him to gather reinforcements, resulting in Ramsay and Bolton men slaying Rodrik, White Harbor knights and Hornwoods, along with other northern [Stark](wiki:House_Stark) loyalists, in a [battle at Winterfell](wiki:Battle_at_Winterfell). Theon allows Ramsay into Winterfell, resulting in the slaughtering of the ironborn garrison of Winterfell and [sacking](wiki:Sack_of_Winterfell) the [winter town](wiki:Winter_town).(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Racok66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

Following the betrayal of House Stark by the Boltons and Freys at the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding), the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) legitimizes Ramsay and names his father, Lord Roose Bolton, [Warden of the North](wiki:Warden_of_the_North).(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Rasos72.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) Ramsay presents himself from this point onwards as [Lord of the Hornwood](wiki:Lord_of_the_Hornwood) and heir to the Dreadfort.(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)(wiki:Fighting_in_the_Hornwood.cite_ref-Radwd37.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)

Because Wyman Manderly's son and heir, Ser [Wylis](wiki:Wylis_Manderly), was captured at the [Trident](wiki:Fighti

...[TRUNCATED — body exceeds 8000 char cap]...

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: War of the Five Kings (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Pyrrhic Iron Throne victory (track_b: Result)

## Narrative Arc

Lord [Paxter Redwyne](wiki:Paxter_Redwyne) leads the [Redwyne fleet](wiki:Redwyne_fleet) in transporting two thousand [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) men(wiki:Siege_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Raffc16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) from [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) across [Blackwater Bay](wiki:Blackwater_Bay) to the [isle of Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone_(island)). Paxter has his men encamp before the walls of the [castle of Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone).(wiki:Siege_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Raffc32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) He intends to take the castle bloodlessly by either starving the defenders out or creating a breach in the walls through mining and forcing the defenders to surrender.

With the development of [ironmen](wiki:Ironmen) raiding the [Reach](wiki:Reach) after the [taking of the Shields](wiki:Taking_of_the_Shields), there is more pressure on the besiegers to end the conflict quickly to free up the Redwyne fleet. Ser [Loras Tyrell](wiki:Loras_Tyrell) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) successfully asks Queen Regent [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) for the command, as he believes Paxter is taking too long. Cersei agrees, hoping that Loras will die in the battle.(wiki:Siege_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Raffc32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3)

## Aftermath

Ser [Loras Tyrell](wiki:Loras_Tyrell) is reportedly gravely wounded(wiki:Siege_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Raffc36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3) and dying on [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone_(island)).(wiki:Siege_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Radwd54.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) According to Lord [Mace Tyrell](wiki:Mace_Tyrell), his son's men found no wealth or [dragon eggs](wiki:Dragon_egg) on the isle.(wiki:Siege_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Radwdepilogue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

With Dragonstone taken, Lord [Paxter Redwyne](wiki:Paxter_Redwyne) (now [grand admiral](wiki:Master_of_ships)) is free to return to the [Reach](wiki:Reach) with the [Arbor fleet](wiki:Redwyne_fleet) to defend against King [Euron Greyjoy](wiki:Euron_Greyjoy)'s ironborn. It also removes the last direct threat to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) and causes further damage to [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon)'s already failing cause.

## Quotes

> Only a few fishing boats remain on [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone_(island)) to oppose [Redwyne's](wiki:Paxter_Redwyne) landing. [The castle](wiki:Dragonstone) may hold for some time, but once we have the port we can cut the garrison off from [the sea](wiki:Blackwater_Bay).(wiki:Siege_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Raffc17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)
>
> —[Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) to [Orton Merryweather](wiki:Orton_Merryweather)

> **Loras**: It will take half a year or more to starve [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone) into submission, as Lord [Paxter](wiki:Paxter_Redwyne) means to do. Give me the command, Your Grace. The castle will be yours within a fortnight if I have to tear it down with my bare hands. **Cersei**: Your courage takes my breath away, Ser Loras.(wiki:Siege_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Raffc32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4)
>
> —[Loras Tyrell](wiki:Loras_Tyrell) and [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister)

## Identity

The night [Robb Stark](robb-stark) condemned [Lord Rickard Karstark](rickard-karstark) to death for [murdering the Lannister and Frey captives](karstark-murders-prisoners-at-riverrun), the Karstark host melted away from [Riverrun](riverrun) — "near three hundred riders and twice as many mounts," all the mounted strength of Karhold, stealing off into the rain in ones and twos and then in larger groups. The Blackfish rode out after them but expected to bring back few. With the Karstark foot away under [Roose Bolton](roose-bolton) likely to turn at the news of their liege's [execution](execution-of-rickard-karstark) as well, Robb's army was gutted of a major contingent at the worst possible moment. The loss deepened his dependence on a renewed alliance with [House Frey](house-frey) — the reconciliation that would draw him to the Twins and the [Red Wedding](red-wedding).

## Edges

(Causal/role edges live in `graph/edges/edges.jsonl`, S123 WO5K-remainder track / J7. Set in motion by [Rickard Karstark's murder of the captives](karstark-murders-prisoners-at-riverrun) (CAUSES, Tier-2) — the act doomed their lord, and anticipating Robb's certain justice the riders fled that night, before the dawn [execution](execution-of-rickard-karstark), rather than serve the king who would kill him. The murders are the prior root cause of both the execution and this desertion. The desertion ENABLES [the Red Wedding conspiracy](red-wedding-conspiracy) (Tier-2) — Robb's gutted host deepens his dependence on the desperate Frey reconciliation that becomes the trap. Closes the gap between two already-built B1 segments: `karstark-murders-prisoners CAUSES execution-of-rickard-karstark` and `robb-weds-jeyne TRIGGERS red-wedding-conspiracy`.)

## Origins

During the [Dance of the Dragons](wiki:Dance_of_the_Dragons), [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) of the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) supported the [greens](wiki:Greens), while [House Stark](wiki:House_Stark) of the [north](wiki:North) and most of the disorganized lords of the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) supported the [blacks](wiki:Blacks). Lord [Jason Lannister](wiki:Jason_Lannister) led an army eastward in support of [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen), but Jason was killed by [Pate of Longleaf](wiki:Pate_of_Longleaf) in the [Battle at the Red Fork](wiki:Battle_at_the_Red_Fork)(wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) and Ser [Adrian Tarbeck](wiki:Adrian_Tarbeck) was later slain by Ser [Harry Penny](wiki:Harry_Penny).(wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) The Lannister army continued slowly because of the age and infirmity of its wounded new commander, Lord [Humfrey Lefford](wiki:Humfrey_Lefford).(wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

When the [westermen](wiki:Westermen) reached the western shore of the [Gods Eye](wiki:Gods_Eye), Lord Lefford discovered he was opposed by Lord [Roderick Dustin](wiki:Roderick_Dustin) with two thousand [Winter Wolves](wiki:Winter_Wolves) (all of them mounted), Lord [Forrest Frey](wiki:Forrest_Frey) with two hundred [knights](wiki:Knight) and six hundred infantrymen, and [Robb Rivers](wiki:Robb_Rivers) with three hundred [archers](wiki:Archer) from [Raventree Hall](wiki:Raventree_Hall). The westermen were quickly faced with another threat from the south, when Ser [Pate of Longleaf](wiki:Pate_of_Longleaf) arrived with survivors from previous battles in the war (possibly from the [Pipers](wiki:House_Piper) and [Vances](wiki:House_Vance)),(wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore.cite_ref-Combatant_3-0) as well as Lords [Bigglestone](wiki:Lord_Bigglestone), [Chambers](wiki:Lord_Chambers), and [Perryn](wiki:Lord_Perryn). The cautious Humfrey did not rush to face either enemy force, and thus be caught out of position in the field, but took up a defensive position with his back to the lakeshore. Humfrey then sent [ravens](wiki:Raven) to nearby [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal) to seek assistance from Prince [Aemond Targaryen](wiki:Aemond_Targaryen), but Red Robb shot down the dozen ravens. The blacks received further reinforcement with the arrival of more [riverlords](wiki:Riverlords) the following day, including Ser [Garibald Grey](wiki:Garibald_Grey), Lord [Jon Charlton](wiki:Jon_Charlton), and young Lord [Benjicot Blackwood](wiki:Benjicot_Blackwood).(wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

Despite cornering the Lannister army, the black commanders now faced a difficult decision: the Lannisters had pulled back into a defensive position behind columns of disciplined pikemen, so a direct assault would still result in heavy losses. Nonetheless, they could not simply wait to starve out the Lannister army, because Aemond's host would eventually realize that communications had ceased, and rush to the aid of the Lannisters - bringing Aemond's [dragon](wiki:Dragon) [Vhagar](wiki:Vhagar). Lord Dustin and the other black commanders therefore chose to rush the Lannister position while they still could.(wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

## Narrative Arc

The army of westermen were attacked at dawn the next morning. The Winter Wolves led the attack, their cavalry charging the Lannister spearmen five times, but suffering grievous losses in the attempts. Attacked by the blacks from three sides, the Lannister soldiers were gradually forced back into the Gods Eye. Hundreds died fighting amongst the lake's reeds, and hundreds of others drowned while trying to escape.(wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)

The battle degenerated into carnage, and was ultimately the bloodiest land battle of the entire war; the high number of casualties led to it being called the "Fishfeed" by its participants. The blacks lost Lords Frey, Bigglestone, and Charlton, and two-thirds of the northern soldiers were killed or wounded. The greens lost their commander, Lord Lefford, as well as Lords [Swyft](wiki:Lord_Swyft) and [Reyne](wiki:Lord_Reyne) and the knights [Clarent Crakehall](wiki:Clarent_Crakehall) and [Emory Hill](wiki:Emory_Hill). Young Lord Blackwood wept when he saw all of the dead.(wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)

## Aftermath

The Lannisters' main army was totally destroyed in the battle, the flower of knighthood from all the westerlands cut down in a single day. With only skeleton forces left to defend their home territory, [Dalton Greyjoy](wiki:Dalton_Greyjoy) opportunistically allied with Rhaenyra so the [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn) could plunder the coasts of the westerlands, sacking [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport) but failing to take [Casterly Rock](wiki:Casterly_Rock) itself. Despite their disproportionate victory, the black armies in the riverlands were also badly mauled in the bloodbath.

In response to the greens' crushing defeat, Prince Aemond and Ser [Criston Cole](wiki:Criston_Cole) abandoned Harrenhal. Aemond waged a guerilla war atop [Vhagar](wiki:Vhagar), and Criston headed south to find Lord [Ormund Hightower](wiki:Ormund_Hightower). The victorious rivermen avoided facing Criston's host directly, but wore him down with ambushes and scorched earth tactics, which included arranging the bodies of fallen soldiers from the Fishfeed in gruesome displays to frighten Criston's host (causing many to desert). The rivermen (and few surviving Winter Wolves) eventually cornered Criston's depleted army in the [Butcher's Ball](wiki:Butcher's_Ball).(wiki:Battle_by_the_Lakeshore.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)

## Origins

After escaping the [fall of King's Landing](wiki:Fall_of_King's_Landing), King [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen) hid on [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone_(island)), the island stronghold of his rival, Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen). He convinced several [blacks](wiki:Blacks), including Ser [Alfred Broome](wiki:Alfred_Broome) and Ser [Marston Waters](wiki:Marston_Waters), to defect to the [greens](wiki:Greens) and help him take the island, with the assistance of [Tom Tanglebeard](wiki:Tom_Tanglebeard) and [Tom Tangletongue](wiki:Tom_Tangletongue). Marston secured the [ravens](wiki:Raven), preventing Grand Maester [Gerardys](wiki:Gerardys) from sending a warning, while Alfred slew the castellan of Dragonstone, Ser [Robert Quince](wiki:Robert_Quince).(wiki:Fall_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

During the coup, Aegon fought Lady [Baela Targaryen](wiki:Baela_Targaryen), both mounted on their dragons [Sunfyre](wiki:Sunfyre) and [Moondancer](wiki:Moondancer), respectively. The dragons fought in the pre-dawn sky above the castle, eventually slamming into the yard still fighting. Aegon jumped from the saddle twenty feet from the ground, but Baela crashed to the ground with Moondancer. Marston stopped Alfred from slaying the burned girl as she crawled away from her dying dragon, and Tom Tangletongue carried her to the maester.(wiki:Fall_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

Aegon II shattered his legs in a fall during the fight, but he refused [milk of the poppy](wiki:Milk_of_the_poppy) when [Gerardys](wiki:Gerardys) offered it due to fear of Gerardys might poison him and because of his experience with it during his recovery after [Rook's Rest](wiki:Battle_at_Rook's_Rest). Instead, Aegon ordered Gerardys to be fed to Sunfyre, though a part to remain to greet his sister.(wiki:Fall_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

## Aftermath

[Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone) was now in the control of King [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen) and the [greens](wiki:Greens). [Baela Targaryen](wiki:Baela_Targaryen), Aegon, and his [dragon](wiki:Dragon), [Sunfyre](wiki:Sunfyre), were seriously injured during the aerial duel over the island. The charred body of [Robert Quince](wiki:Robert_Quince), Dragonstone's steward, [master-at-arms](wiki:Master-at-arms), captain of the guards, and the upper torso of Grand Maester [Gerardys](wiki:Gerardys) were hanged from the castle battlements near the gate to greet Aegon's sister when she arrived.(wiki:Fall_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

When Rhaenyra eventually [returned to Dragonstone](wiki:Flight_to_Dragonstone), she and her followers were quickly defeated by Ser [Alfred Broome](wiki:Alfred_Broome)'s men.(wiki:Fall_of_Dragonstone.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_overthrown.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)
